extraction.one_line_summary: BATTAGLIN Enrico won from a breakaway that was caught inside 3.5km, surviving the chaotic final kilometer positioning battle.
tactical_patterns: KIRYIENKA's tempo control brought breakaway from 5min to caught in just 5km, revealing Sky's pacing calibration for similar profiles.
VIVIANI caught behind main group with delayed team response, suggesting Quick-Step communication gap under echelon-like splits.
Sky positioned FROOME aggressively inside 3km despite GC riders typically waiting, signaling defensive marking strategy on rolling finishes.
Breakaway survived to 3.5km then collapsed instantly, indicating critical threshold where chasing effort exceeds holding advantage on this terrain.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: caught off position requiring multiple teammates to pace back, uncharacteristic positioning error from sprint specialist
rider_signals.BATTAGLIN Enrico: positive: won from doomed breakaway by surviving positioning chaos in final kilometer better than stronger names
rider_signals.KIRYIENKA Vasil: positive: tempo riding erased 5-minute gap in 5km segment, demonstrating diesel engine value on rolling terrain
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: Astana riding desperately to bring him back to peloton inside 3km, worrying positioning for GC contender
rider_signals.ULISSI Diego: positive: UAE leading out attacks with 1km remaining on uphill finish, showing sprint-climb hybrid ambitions
rider_signals.PUCCIO Salvatore: positive: took bonus seconds in third place finish, Team Sky gathering time splits aggressively early in race
rider_signals.VISCONTI Giovanni: positive: second place suggests strong finishing kick on short uphill drag after positioning chaos
